data.narrative:
SkySafe is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 8 private companies operating in the same sector and stage, alongside 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The private comp universe includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion, with valuations ranging from $300M to $1.5B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy base of $50M, applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $95M. The peer-median method examined 8 same-sector, same-stage private companies and derived a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B across the cohort. These approaches produced materially divergent results, reflecting fundamental uncertainty around SkySafe's positioning relative to established defense-tech entrants.

The triangulated valuation range is $81M (low), $610M (median), and $1.3B (high). The wide spread—spanning more than 15x between floor and ceiling—underscores the sensitivity of this analysis to assumptions about comparable applicability and growth trajectory. Key uncertainties include whether SkySafe's technology, market traction, or team composition justifies positioning toward the upper or lower bound of peer valuations, and whether stage-progression or peer-median methodology better captures market conditions at the time of next financing.
